title: Kraft paper journal cover
materials: kraft paper (80-120 gsm brown kraft, or recycled grocery bag paper)
pva-regular or a glue stick (for a pocket or insert attachment)
sources: SYNTHESISED — kraft paper journals are a contemporary craft. No pre-1928 PD source.
notes: A removable kraft-paper wrap-around cover for a standard staple-bound or sewn-section notebook. Converts a plain notebook into a decorated object and protects it. Covers: measuring and cutting the kraft paper to wrap the notebook with flaps on all four edges, scoring the spine fold with a bone folder for a clean crease, folding the fore-edge flap to lock the cover in place (no glue needed for the basic version), adding a bookmark ribbon, decorating with washi tape, stamps, or drawn patterns. Optional: adding a small pocket inside the back flap for storing small items. The technique transfers to any mass-market notebook.
